description: Creates a 3D angle arc between two rays or calculates angle between geometric objects. For plane-plane (dihedral) angles, draws perpendicular rays in each plane from the line of intersection. For line-plane angles, draws arc between the line direction and its projection onto the plane. Can be anchored and projected in 3D multi-select operations.
utility: Draw or calculate an angle in 3D space between rays, vectors, lines, or planes

arguments.name: vertex_x
arguments.description: X coordinate of the angle vertex

arguments.name: vertex_y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the angle vertex

arguments.name: vertex_z
arguments.description: Z coordinate of the angle vertex

arguments.name: point1_x
arguments.description: X coordinate of the first ray endpoint

arguments.name: point1_y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the first ray endpoint

arguments.name: point1_z
arguments.description: Z coordinate of the first ray endpoint

arguments.name: point2_x
arguments.description: X coordinate of the second ray endpoint

arguments.name: point2_y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the second ray endpoint

arguments.name: point2_z
arguments.description: Z coordinate of the second ray endpoint

alternates.syntax: angle3d(graph, vertex_x, vertex_y, vertex_z, point1_x, point1_y, point1_z, point2_x, point2_y, point2_z, radius)
alternates.description: Specify a custom radius for the angle arc
Angle arc from vertex point3d through two arm endpoint point3d references
Calculate angle between two vector3d expressions
Calculate angle between two line3d expressions
Dihedral angle between two planes. Draws perpendicular rays from the line of intersection
Angle between a line and plane. Draws arc between line direction and its projection onto the plane

examples.scenario: Angle between vectors in scalar triple product
angle between two rays from vertex

examples.expression:
pl1 = plane3d(G, "x + y = 2", type(cartesian))
pl2 = plane3d(G, "z = 0", type(cartesian))
angle3d(G, pl1, pl2)

L = line3d(G, point3d(G, 0, 0, 0), point3d(G, 1, 1, 1))
P = plane3d(G, "z = 0", type(cartesian))
angle3d(G, L, P)

examples.scenario: Spherical coordinates: theta arc (polar angle from z-axis to radial vector)
examples.expression:
pOrigin = point3d(G, 0, 0, 0)
P = point3d(G, r, theta, phi, type(spherical))
thetaArc = angle3d(G, pOrigin, point3d(G, 0, 0, r), P, arcsize(14), c(black), arrowhead(end), s(0.03))

examples.scenario: Spherical coordinates: phi arc (azimuthal angle in xy-plane)
examples.expression:
pOrigin = point3d(G, 0, 0, 0)
P = point3d(G, r, theta, phi, type(spherical))
xyPlane = plane3d(G, "z = 0", type(cartesian), defer())
pProj = project3d(xyPlane, P, defer())
phiArc = angle3d(G, pOrigin, point3d(G, r, 0, 0), pProj, arcsize(10), c(black), arrowhead(end), s(0.03))
